title: Predicting a historic storm earlier with WeatherNext
summary: WeatherNext, Google DeepMind's global weather forecasting AI model, successfully predicted Hurricane Melissa's intensity and track in October 2025, providing advance warning days before a Category 5 landfall in Jamaica. The system enabled meteorologists and authorities to issue evacuation warnings and protect communities through high-confidence early signals.
writeup: Google's WeatherNext AI model demonstrated operational hurricane forecasting capability during Hurricane Melissa in 2025, predicting the storm's rapid intensification and Jamaican landfall three days before impact with greater accuracy than prior models. Forecasters at the time faced two divergent scenarios: a weak storm over Haiti versus a Category 5 strike on Jamaica. WeatherNext resolved that uncertainty early enough for emergency managers to issue life-threatening hazard warnings and direct evacuations, with Jamaican officials crediting the advance notice with saving lives and livelihoods. The video makes the case that AI weather models are transitioning from research tools to standard components of operational forecast workflows at agencies like the National Hurricane Center.
key_points: WeatherNext predicted Hurricane Melissa's Category 5 intensification and Jamaican landfall three days in advance, outperforming previous models on a scenario where forecasters were initially split between two very different outcomes.

The model's high-confidence signals were used to issue urgent public warnings about life-threatening hazards days before landfall, giving residents time to evacuate targeted areas before the storm arrived.

Jamaican emergency management officials credited the early warning directly with saving lives and protecting livelihoods, providing concrete evidence of AI-driven forecast value in an operational setting.

The National Hurricane Center stated that WeatherNext and similar AI models will become part of its routine forecast toolkit going forward, signaling a shift from experimental use to standard operational integration.
